name: bridge_open_edge_rows
description: mesh.bridgeTool (task 0395) — EDGE-selection Bridge contract when the selected edges form OPEN chains/rows instead of exactly 2 closed cycles. Reported bug: a cube with 2 adjacent faces deleted (8v/4f/11e), selecting the 4 boundary edges away from the two connector edges (two 2-edge open arcs) makes Bridge silently no-op in vibe3d (bridge.d:91 requires exactly 2 CLOSED cycles via extractSelectedEdgeCycles). Reference-editor capture (task 0395, live, worker_id=31) shows the reference tool accepts open rows directly: it pairs the two chains by NEAREST-ENDPOINT PROXIMITY (not creation/selection order — verified via an asymmetric mesh where index-order pairing and proximity pairing predict different geometry, see 'pairing_proximity' case), applies the SAME Segments interior-ring law already verified for closed-loop/polygon-mode Bridge (spans=max(1,segments), interior rings lerped at t=i/spans), fans/triangulates when the two rows have unequal edge counts (excess edges on the longer row are trimmed into end-triangles toward the shorter row's near endpoint; the aligned middle edges bridge 1:1), and no-ops on a single open chain with nothing else selected (not an error, not a self-close).
source: live reference-editor capture, task 0395 (private capture harness: tools/local/modo_diff/atlas/bridge_openrows_build.py + bridge_openrows_capture.py, worker_id=31)

provenance.notes: Explicit-geometry construction (item.create mesh -> geo.vertices.new/geo.polygons.new -> geo.setMeshEdits(), the proven bridge_ngon_build.py recipe) with edges selected by POSITION match (not index, since index stability across a mesh-edit commit is not guaranteed by the Python API). Every case's expected_vertices/expected_after below is copied verbatim from the captured post-tool dump, not hand-derived. NOTE (not encoded as a fixture, needs a 'connect'/Auto-Connection attribute vibe3d does not have yet): on the reported-bug's specific topology (an internal hole in an otherwise-closed box), the reference tool's Auto Connection default (connect=true) silently OVERRIDES an explicit Segments>1 request back down to spans=1 to keep the patch flush with the surrounding surface; with connect=false (or on a non-enclosed/disjoint topology, e.g. this fixture's other cases) the requested Segments value is honored exactly. Deferred — flagged for whoever implements Auto Connection, not required for the reported bug (which uses the default segments=1).

cases.name: reported_bug_two_open_arcs_equal
cases.description: Exact owner repro (:9000, 2026-07-14): cube minus 2 adjacent faces (right x=+0.5, back y=+0.5), select the 4 boundary edges away from the two connector edges -- two 2-edge open arcs of EQUAL length. Reference tool bridges them into 2 new quads reusing the EXISTING boundary vertices (span=1, no interior rings) -- it literally reconstructs the two deleted faces. This is the case vibe3d's edge.bridge command currently no-ops on (extractSelectedEdgeCycles requires 2 CLOSED cycles); this fixture is the must-pass regression for the bug report.

cases.name: two_open_rows_disjoint_default
cases.description: Two open 2-edge/3-vertex rows on a DISJOINT (non-enclosed) topology -- a different shape than the cube-hole case above, confirming open-row bridging is general and not an artifact of the specific hole geometry. Default segments=1: span=1, no interior rings, 2 new quads connecting the rows' existing vertices 1:1 by position (0<->0, 1<->1, 2<->2 along x).

cases.name: two_open_rows_segments2
cases.description: Same disjoint 2-row mesh as above, Segments=2 -- the closed-form Segments law already verified for polygon-mode/closed-loop Bridge (spans=max(1,segments); interior rings linearly interpolated at t=i/spans) applies IDENTICALLY to open-row edge-mode bridging: 1 interior ring at t=0.5 (y=0.5, lerped between the y=0 and y=1 rows), 3 new vertices, 4 new quads (2 spans x 2 row-edges).

cases.name: pairing_proximity_not_selection_order
cases.description: DISCRIMINATING case (task 0395): row A's 2 edges are selected in natural x-increasing order (0->1->2); row B's 2 edges are on the SAME x positions but row B's vertices were built/selected in the SPATIALLY REVERSED order (b0 at x=2, b1 at x=1, b2 at x=0), walked b0->b1->b2. A naive creation/selection-INDEX pairing (A's i-th selected edge endpoint <-> B's i-th) would pair a0(x=0) with b0(x=2) and a2(x=2) with b0's far end at x=0 -- for THIS symmetric-about-x=1 layout that degenerate rule collapses ALL 3 interior-ring (segments=2) vertices onto x=1 (midpoint of 0&2 is 1, midpoint of 1&1 is 1, midpoint of 2&0 is 1). The reference tool instead pairs by NEAREST-ENDPOINT PROXIMITY regardless of creation/selection order (auto-detecting each chain's correct walk direction) -- the captured interior-ring vertices sit at x=0, x=1, x=2 (three DISTINCT positions, not collapsed), proving proximity pairing. A vibe3d port that pairs by raw index/selection order instead of endpoint proximity will fail this fixture's expected_vertices (collapsed-to-x=1 interior ring) even though it may pass the index-aligned cases above.

cases.name: unequal_length_rows_fan
cases.description: Row A = 3 edges/4 verts (x=0,1,2,3, y=0); row B = 1 edge/2 verts (x=0,3, y=1), same total span. Reference tool does NOT refuse and does NOT require equal edge counts: it fans/triangulates -- the two OUTER edges of the longer row (a0-a1 and a2-a3) each become a triangle with the nearest endpoint of the shorter row (b0 at x=0, b1 at x=3 respectively), and the MIDDLE edge of the longer row (a1-a2) bridges as a quad against the shorter row's full single edge (b0-b1). No new vertices; 3 new faces (2 triangles + 1 quad) replace the would-be 'need equal counts' refusal. Symmetric under swapping which row is longer (unequal_A1_B3 companion capture, not separately fixture'd here, reproduces the mirror image bit-for-bit).

cases.name: single_open_chain_noop
cases.description: A SINGLE open 2-edge chain selected, nothing else -- the reference tool leaves geometry completely UNCHANGED (no error, no self-closing into a loop, no new faces). Contrast with the 2-open-arc cases above: Bridge needs a SECOND selected group to bridge to; one group alone is a legitimate no-op, not a bug. (Distinguishes from the reported bug, where TWO groups were selected but vibe3d's closed-cycle-only gate rejected them anyway.)
